hub_subtitle: Business English for HR leadership in international meetings

lesson.menu_title: When It Was Happening — Describing Unfolding Situations in Meetings and Crisis Moments
lesson.menu_desc: Describing background and interrupted past actions with the past continuous -- 31 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 9 -- When It Was Happening -- Past Continuous
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Murielle Xavier | Aula 9 -- When It Was Happening

listenings.text: Hi Murielle, it's Liam. Let me tell you what happened last Friday, because it was quite a morning. At nine o'clock, the whole team was working normally. I was reviewing the payroll, two managers were interviewing candidates, and Sofia was preparing the report. Then, while we were working, the main system suddenly crashed. It was a complete breakdown. When the system went down, three employees were in the middle of a handover, and they lost their notes. People were calling me from every department, and I was trying to stay calm. While the IT team was fixing the problem, I was handling the complaints. It took two hours, but we resolved it. The good news is that nobody panicked, and the crisis is over now. I am sending you a short incident report. Talk soon.

Good morning, Murielle. This is Diana from London, with an update on the dispute in the sales team. Last week, while I was investigating the grievance, I spoke to several witnesses. Here is what I learned. On Monday afternoon, two employees were arguing in the meeting room when the manager walked in. According to the witnesses, one person was raising his voice while the other was taking notes for a complaint. It was not a case of serious misconduct, but the disruption was real. While we were reviewing the emails, we noticed the problem had started weeks earlier. The good news is that we resolved the dispute yesterday, and both employees agreed to a new arrangement. The investigation is closed. I am sending you the full report now. Thank you.
